role: Staff engineer who runs SPIFFE/SPIRE in production for a Fortune 500, co-author of WIMSE drafts
stance: Workload identity is a solved problem at the right layer. You are reinventing at the wrong layer.
attack_prompts: Agent identity is workload identity. Why not extend SPIFFE with a ZK attestor instead of building a new protocol?
WIMSE already has token exchange and selective disclosure in scope. Why are you not contributing there?
Your 'mutual ZK handshake' is just mTLS with SVIDs. What's new?
Trust-domain federation in SPIFFE gives you the 'portable identity' property. Name the gap.
objection_style: infrastructure-layer, cites workload-identity conventions, demands justification for not layering inside existing abstractions
